title: Reduce Stereotypical Bias: - Pause and Question: Before Making Assumptions, Ask Yourself, “what Evidence
subtitle: Break Free from Stereotypes
description:
To reduce stereotypical bias:
- Pause and question: Before making assumptions, ask yourself, “What evidence do I have?”
- Focus on individuals: Get to know people beyond labels and categories.
- Seek diverse perspectives: Actively listen to stories and views different from your own.

Example: Instead of assuming someone’s skills based on their background, ask them about their experiences directly.

howTo.title: Pause and Question
howTo.summary: Before making assumptions, notice the impulse to categorize, take a brief pause, and ask yourself what evidence you have to support your judgment.

howTo.title: Engage with Individuals
howTo.summary: Focus on getting to know people beyond their labels. Ask direct questions about their experiences to gain a clearer understanding.

howTo.title: Seek Diverse Perspectives
howTo.summary: Actively listen to stories and viewpoints that differ from your own. This exposure helps challenge and reduce stereotypical biases.

howTo.title: Track Your Progress
howTo.summary: Count the number of times you pause and question assumptions each day. Log these metrics to measure improvement over time.

howTo.title: Check in with LifeOS Brali app
howTo.summary: Log your results and reflections in the Brali LifeOS app to track your progress and insights. Visit https://metalhatscats.com/life-os/reduce-stereotype-bias-coach/.
